title: Fully analogue reinforcement learning with memristors
data_sources: HfOx RRAM crossbar array (Portner et al. 2025)

data_scope.benchmark_tasks: T-maze (~50 episodes to convergence); continuous Morris water-maze (hundreds of trials)
data_scope.article_type: News & Views commentary on Portner et al. (doi:10.1038/s42256-025-01149-w)
data_scope.primary_data_source: Companion research article by Portner et al. (doi:10.1038/s42256-025-01149-w)
data_scope.data_availability: No independent dataset released; all empirical results from Portner et al.

core_conclusion: An actor-critic reinforcement learning network implemented fully in hafnium-oxide memristor crossbar arrays performs storage, inference, temporal-difference error computation, and weight updates entirely in analogue hardware without digital multiply-accumulate units or external memory traffic, demonstrated on T-maze (27 devices, ~50 episodes) and Morris water-maze (~1000 synapses, hundreds of trials) benchmarks.
why_it_matters: First fully analogue on-chip RL system eliminating the digital gap in neuromorphic RL; Ohm's and Kirchhoff's laws perform all algebra inside the array; self-correcting noise tolerance via online TD recomputation; bridges hardware engineering to biological learning benchmarks; pathway to sustainable battery-powered edge AI.
why_nature: Paradigm-shifting result beyond prior hybrid digital-analogue systems; unifies neuroscience, physics, and AI in a single substrate; addresses the AI energy crisis with a physics-first remedy; published as invited News & Views commentary on a landmark companion research article.
added_value_to_my_research: Analogue TD rule on graph substrate -> hardware-native facility-location optimization bypassing digital simulation bottleneck in 2SFCA scaling
In-memory Bayesian uncertainty via memristor stochasticity -> active spatiotemporal sampling for spatial data collection
Physical backpropagation (Training of PNNs) -> hardware-accelerated agent-based Monte Carlo spatial simulation
CMOS-compatible synaptic transistors -> analogue RL co-processor accelerating EV charging / facility-siting inner-loop policy evaluation
Reward-magnitude calibration -> physics-grounded reward-design heuristic linking spatial utility scale to hardware learning rate

related.title: Resistive memory-based zero-shot liquid state machine for multimodal event data learning
related.relation: RRAM-based in-memory temporal processing; extends memristor RL to graph-structured spatial inputs

related.title: Deep Bayesian active learning using in-memory computing hardware
related.relation: in-memory Bayesian uncertainty estimation; complements analogue TD with exploration signal for active spatial data collection

related.title: Training of physical neural networks
related.relation: physical backpropagation framework; resolves the remaining digital-module dependency in the memristor RL system

related.title: Synaptic and neural behaviours in a standard silicon transistor
related.relation: CMOS-compatible synaptic plasticity; pathway to foundry-scalable analogue RL for large-scale urban optimization

related.title: Reward magnitude determines reinforcement learning efficiency
related.relation: reward-scale effect on RL convergence; informs analogue voltage-amplitude calibration for the TD circuit

figures.caption: On-chip in-memory reinforcement learning on analogue memristors — actor and critic weights stored as crossbar conductances; TD error computed and weight updates applied entirely in hardware
